evals.prompt: I want to add a 'duplicate project' feature. Users should be able to clone an existing project with all its tasks and members. Where should it live?
evals.expected_output: Routes the user through Step 1 (context), Step 2 (language alignment: project, task, member, duplicate), Step 3 (decisions: deep vs shallow clone, naming collision, permissions), Step 4 ('Blueprint ready'), Step 4.5 (pressure-test), Step 4.6 (self-review), Step 5 (plan), Step 5.5 (persistence check), Step 6 (execution mode menu). Does NOT skip directly to writing code or proposing architecture without alignment.
expectations: Asks language-alignment questions before proposing implementation
Surfaces at least 3 meaningful decisions (data ownership, naming, permissions, etc.)
Eventually says 'Blueprint ready' or equivalent gate before producing the plan
Presents an implementation plan with files/steps, not just a description
After plan confirmation, presents the Step 6 execution-mode menu with 4 options
Does NOT skip Step 6 and jump straight into code without a mode choice

evals.prompt: Tiny typo fix: the 'Logiin' heading should be 'Login'.
evals.expected_output: Skips architect entirely. Notes it's a trivial fix and proceeds without a multi-step plan, language alignment, or execution mode menu.
expectations: Does NOT run Steps 1-6 of architect
Recognizes this as a one-line fix and skips the skill
Does NOT ask language-alignment questions or propose multi-file changes
May mention to just make the change

evals.prompt: We need to add role-based access control across the app. 6 user roles, ~40 protected routes, complex permission inheritance. Will take weeks.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the scope and triggers persistence mode (Step 5.5). Writes design + plan to docs/designs/ and docs/plans/. Surfaces many decisions. Does NOT try to cover everything inline in chat.
expectations: Recognizes this is large enough to warrant persistence mode
Mentions writing a design doc to docs/designs/
Mentions writing a plan doc to docs/plans/
Surfaces decisions about RBAC model (role hierarchy, permission inheritance, route guard placement, etc.)
Uses delta notation or acknowledges that future updates will use ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED markers
Does NOT try to plan the whole thing inline in one chat response
